title: News — Nick Herbert
text_excerpt: News — Nick Herbert About News Work Contact Back Nick's biography Back Policing Countryside LGBT TB Government reform Why Vote Conservative? About Nick's biography News Work Policing Countryside LGBT TB Government reform Why Vote Conservative? Contact Nick becomes motoring correspondent of The Field Magazine Nick’s first review features the ultimate Range Rover — the Holland & Holland Edition by Overfinch Read More News releases Nick Herbert December 11, 2025 The Field , Cars Activists have their sights set on horse racing In an article in the Daily Telegraph, Nick warns that greyhound racing is already being banned in Wales and Scotland, but animal rights groups want to go further. Read More Articles Nick Herbert November 14, 2025 racing , hunting , countryside alliance , country sports Nick says that with leadership a vaccine could transform the fight against TB Speaking at the G20 Health Ministers’ meeting in South Africa, Nick warned that it would be a tragedy if the world failed to deliver an effective new TB vaccine. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert November 6, 2025 TB , Global TB Caucus Nick launches new Commission on Police Leadership Nick will co-chair the review with Lord Blunkett to examine police leadership at all levels with the aim of ensuring that policing can respond to evolving threats and challenges and meet public expectations. Read More News releases Nick Herbert October 23, 2025 police The system of recording 'non-crime hate incidents' is no longer fit for purpose In the House of Lords Nick says that an official review has concluded that reform is needed to ensure that policing can focus on genuine harm and risk within communities. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert October 16, 2025 police No so-called safeguard will persuade me that the assisted suicide Bill is safe or right Nick speaks in the Second Reading debate in the House of Lords on the Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert September 12, 2025 TB must remain a global health priority for the Government In questions in the House of Lords Nick points out that the world remains dramatically off-target to beat TB by 2030. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert July 21, 2025 TB , Global TB Caucus The proposed Holocaust learning centre is in the wrong location Nick speaks at Report Stage of the Holocaust Memorial Bill in the House of Lords. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert June 11, 2025 Holocaust Memorial , LGBT Nick renews calls for independent review on transgender policy In the House of Lords, Nick says that it is time to look at transgender issues dispassionately and carefully, and to detoxify the debate. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert May 1, 2025 LGBT 'Yes, we can end TB': Nick's message on World TB Day In a video message Nick says that while the fight to beat the world’s deadliest infectious disease faces new challenges there is still hope that we can beat TB. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert March 24, 2025 TB , Global TB Caucus , Global Fund We must maintain government programmes to end TB Ahead of World TB Day Nick asked a question in the House of Lords about the need for the Government to maintain TB programmes when the US has cut funding. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert March 20, 2025 TB , Global TB Caucus , Global Fund Change is no longer a choice In an article in The Field, Nick warns that the threat to ban trail hunting necessitates going further and faster to demonstrate that the sport is being properly conducted. Read More Articles Nick Herbert February 5, 2025 hunting , Countryside Alliance Nick addresses Nigerian Parliament on urgency of tackling TB Together with his co-founder of the Global TB Caucus and Africa Region Co-Chair, Hon Stephen Mule MP from Kenya, Nick spoke to MPs in the Green Chamber of the House of Representatives in Abuja. Read More Speeches Nick Herbert December 14, 2024 TB , Global TB Caucus Non-crime hate incidents are undermining trust in officers, says police college head In an interview
